scope_note: QA Fibonacci GL2 Scalar Identity and Pisano Period cert [426]. Primary sources: Lucas, E. (1878) 'Theorie des fonctions numeriques simplement periodiques' American Journal of Mathematics 1(2) pp. 184-240 doi:10.2307/2369308 (Lucas sequences L_n; trace of Fibonacci matrix M^n equals L_n; GL2 invariants of the recurrence matrix); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) pp. 525-532 doi:10.2307/2309169 (Pisano period T(p); Fibonacci periodicity mod primes; foundational for [416]-[425] chain; provides T(p) definition and basic period-divisiblity theorems); Lagrange, J.-L. (1771) 'Demonstration d'un theoreme d'arithmetique' Nouveaux Memoires de l'Academie Royale des Sciences de Berlin pp. 125-138 (multiplicative order divides group order; ord(g) | p-1 for g in (Z/pZ)x; used to bound ord(epsilon) in {1,2,4}). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (A) tr(M^n) = F_{n+1}+F_{n-1} = L_n (Lucas numbers) for n=1..50; det(M^n) = (-1)^n for n=1..50; verified in exact integer arithmetic via fast doubling. (B) For all primes 7<=p<=500: M^{alpha(p)} = epsilon(p)*I_2 mod p where epsilon(p)=F_{alpha(p)-1} mod p; off-diagonal entry F_{alpha(p)} = 0 by definition; diagonal entries F_{alpha(p)+1} = F_{alpha(p)-1} mod p (since F_{alpha+1}=F_alpha+F_{alpha-1} and F_alpha=0); tested 92/92 primes; epsilon order distribution: {ord=1: 32, ord=2: 29, ord=4: 31}. (C) For all primes 7<=p<=300: Pisano period T(p) = alpha(p)*ord_{(Z/pZ)x}(epsilon(p)); derived from M^{alpha*k}=(epsilon^k)*I so M^T=I iff ord(epsilon)|T/alpha; formula verified against direct period walk; tested 59/59 primes. GL2 STRUCTURE: M=[[1,1],[1,0]] in GL2(Z); det(M)=-1; tr(M)=1; char poly = x^2-x-1; eigenvalues phi=(1+sqrt5)/2, psi=(1-sqrt5)/2; M^n=[[F_{n+1},F_n],[F_n,F_{n-1}]]. SCALAR COLLAPSE: (phi/psi)^{alpha(p)}=1 in GL1 (cert [423]) iff phi^{alpha}=psi^{alpha} iff both eigenvalues equal epsilon(p) iff M^{alpha}=epsilon*I in GL2. GL2 upgrade: cert [423] sees the ratio; [426] sees the matrix. PISANO PERIOD FORMULA: T(p)=alpha(p)*ord(epsilon) decomposes the Pisano period into two pure-integer factors: the GL1 rank alpha(p) and the GL2 scalar order ord(epsilon) in {1,2,4}. THEOREM NT: QA layer: fib_fast, rank_of_apparition, pisano_period, mult_order, lucas -- all pure integer. Observer layer: none (all 4 checks are integer comparisons). 4 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-15.
